title: galinsoga quadriradiata subsp. hispida
intro: Galinsoga quadriradiata subsp. hispida, also known as prickly galinsoga, is a plant species that belongs to the sunflower family and is native to Central and South America. Prickly galinsoga has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of this plant include reducing fever, alleviating pain, and treating various skin conditions, such as acne and eczema. Prickly galinsoga can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures for its medicinal benefits, and it is also sometimes used in traditional cooking and as a food ingredient.
